name: GR 92: L'Ametlla de Mar to El Perelló Loop
name_original: L'Ametlla de Mar - El Perelló - Cabo de Santes Creus – circular por el Golfo de Sant Jordi
description: This easy 17.1-mile mountain biking route circles the Golfo de Sant Jordi, offering coastal views and access to Cap de Santes Creus.
region_intro: Mountain biking around L'Ametlla De Mar offers a diverse landscape, blending coastal beauty with inland challenges. The region features 16 kilometers of varied coastline, including untouched coves and scenic paths. Moving inland, the flat expanses of the Ebro Delta Natural Park provide easy cycling routes, while the Sierra de Cardó presents mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain. This combination ensures a wide range of mountain bike trails suitable for different preferences and skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around L'Ametlla De Mar The most popular mountain bike trail is GR 92: L'Ametlla de Mar to El Perelló Loop, an easy 17.1 miles (27.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete, offering coastal views as it circles the Golfo de Sant Jordi. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Coll de Balagué – Sant Jordi Castle loop from l'Ametlla de Mar, a difficult 36.1 miles (58.1 km) path that requires good fitness and advanced riding skills, passing by the historical Sant Jordi Castle. Local mountain bikers also love the Sant Jordi d'Alfama Castle loop from Circuit de Calafat, a 26.2 miles (42.1 km) trail leading through rural environments and forests, often completed in about 4 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around L'Ametlla De Mar is defined by its diverse coastline, the flat expanses of the Ebro Delta, and challenging inland mountainous terrain. The network offers options for easy rides, moderate challenges, and difficult routes for experienced cyclists. The routes in L'Ametlla De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore L'Ametlla De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I expect around L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: L'Ametlla de Mar offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails. You'll find coastal paths with stunning sea views, flat routes through the Ebro Delta Natural Park ideal for relaxed cycling, and more challenging mountainous terrain in areas like the Sierra de Cardó with significant elevation gain. This variety ensures options for all skill levels.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails documented around L'Ametlla de Mar on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 16 easy, 24 moderate, and 11 difficult options.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The flat landscapes of the Ebro Delta Natural Park, located south of L'Ametlla de Mar, are particularly good for 'slow cycling' and offer paths between rice fields. The Greenways (Vía Verda) in the Terres de l'Ebre region, which follow old railway lines, also provide generally easy and scenic cycling through forests and rural environments, perfect for families.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes around L'Ametlla de Mar are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955086979/gr-92-l-ametlla-de-mar-to-el-perello-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: L'Ametlla de Mar to El Perelló Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy coastal option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1397268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Balagué – Sant Jordi Castle loop from l'Ametlla de Mar&lt;/a&gt; offers a more challenging circular ride.

Q: What kind of landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails offer a wealth of sights. Along the coast, you can explore over thirty beaches and coves, and pass by features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Illot Rock and Beach&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Forn Beach&lt;/a&gt;. Inland, you might encounter historical sites like Sant Jordi Castle, or natural wonders such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llop Marí Cove and Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The Ebro Delta is famous for its abundant birdlife, including flamingos.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1397268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Balagué – Sant Jordi Castle loop from l'Ametlla de Mar&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19826768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jordi d'Alfama Castle loop from Circuit de Calafat&lt;/a&gt; both pass by the historical Sant Jordi Castle. Some trails also follow ancient Roman paths or lead through abandoned medieval towns, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The region generally enjoys a mild Mediterranean climate, making mountain biking pleasant for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer comfortable temperatures and less crowded trails. Summer can be hot, especially inland, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that allow dogs?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, many natural paths and rural tracks in the L'Ametlla de Mar area are generally dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Ebro Delta Natural Park, and to ensure they are well-behaved and don't disturb wildlife or other trail users. Always carry water for your dog.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: The mountain bike routes in L'Ametlla de Mar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 50 reviews. Many riders praise the diverse landscapes, from the beautiful coastal views to the challenging inland climbs, and appreciate the variety of options available for different skill levels.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike trails in this region?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly, from shorter, easy rides of around 17 miles (27 km) like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955086979/gr-92-l-ametlla-de-mar-to-el-perello-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: L'Ametlla de Mar to El Perelló Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 2 hours, to more challenging and longer routes up to 36 miles (58 km) that can take over 5 hours to complete, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1397268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Balagué – Sant Jordi Castle loop from l'Ametlla de Mar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trails in L'Ametlla de Mar?
A: L'Ametlla de Mar is a well-equipped coastal town, and parking is generally available in and around the village, especially near popular beaches or starting points for trails. For routes venturing further inland, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or in smaller villages along the route. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.
tips: Relatively difficult access beach located between L'Ametlla de Mar and L'Ampolla. The beach also has the presence of a small lake.
Beach located in an old natural fishing port.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is classified as an easy MTB route, making it suitable for all fitness levels and beginners. It has a low technical difficulty, corresponding to STS S0-S1 levels, meaning you're unlikely to encounter very challenging obstacles or steep, technical descents.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally straightforward. While the broader region can feature diverse landscapes, this specific trail is designed to be accessible. You'll find a mix of paved roads and accessible dirt tracks, offering a pleasant ride through the coastal and inland areas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 27.4 kilometers long with a moderate elevation gain of about 224 meters. On average, it takes around 1 hour and 56 minutes to complete, but this can vary depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, so you can start at various points. A common starting point is in L'Ametlla de Mar. While specific parking details aren't provided, L'Ametlla de Mar and El Perelló are towns with general parking availability for visitors.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque landscapes of the Golfo de Sant Jordi. The route offers a mix of Mediterranean coastal scenery, including stunning beaches, coves, and marine views, as well as glimpses of inland elements like forests. The Cap de Santes Creus area is particularly noted for its breathtaking cliffs and spectacular bays.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest or highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by beautiful coastal spots. You'll encounter the stunning Cala de Santes Creus and the picturesque Natural Port of l'Estany Gras, both offering lovely views and opportunities for a break.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the general area is welcoming to outdoor activities, specific dog regulations for this particular trail are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural areas, and to check local signage for any restrictions regarding pets on beaches or protected areas you might pass through.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Costa Daurada region generally enjoys a mild Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal, offering pleasant temperatures for mountain biking without the intense heat of summer or the cooler, wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable riding and enjoying the coastal views.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain biking trail. It is an open route through public areas and natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Catalunya E29, Camí del Forcall, Urbanització Port de l'Estany, Sendera de la Roca d'en Vidal, and Carrer Racó trails.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'easy' difficulty rating and low technical demands, this mountain biking route is generally suitable for families. The straightforward terrain and beautiful coastal scenery make it an enjoyable outing for riders of varying skill levels, including older children comfortable on a bike.
